actions.intent: Take a safe opener while reading the right-side route as the water-exposed priority.
Advance the exposed puppy's lane rather than assuming the visually lower target is first.
Use side material that does not steal the water-priority tempo.
Clear through the center so the first rescue route remains readable.
Stabilize the board with a harmless edge clear before the water rise.
Keep the dock safe while preserving the route pivot.
Finish the exposed right-side puppy route before turning to the other target.
Pivot through the opened lane toward the remaining puppy.
Complete the safer follow-up rescue after proving the geometry read.

notes: Designer golden for the variation: water exposure is read from the route shape, not only from vertical target position.
